title: WHO DON602: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC
key_message: WHO notes past BVD CFRs in Uganda and DRC have ranged approximately 30% to 50%.
details: Use for initial epidemiologic parameters and outbreak background, but update case counts using later sources.

title: Analysis of past Ebola outbreaks suggests mortality variability and clinical risk factors
key_message: Past Ebola outbreaks show wide mortality variation by setting and study, emphasizing caution in comparing crude CFRs.
details: Useful for interpreting current CFR, especially amid changing ascertainment and suspected-case backlogs.
relevance: CFR interpretation and clinical risk factors.

title: Real-time projections of epidemic transmission and vaccination impact during an Ebola outbreak in eastern DRC
key_message: Branching-process and autoregressive projections were used for real-time Ebola outbreak forecasting under response constraints.
details: Not Bundibugyo-specific but methodologically relevant for short-term forecasts, final-size projections and response-performance indicators.

title: Proportion of Deaths and Clinical Features in Bundibugyo Ebola Virus Infection, Uganda
key_message: Foundational clinical epidemiology paper for the 2007 Bundibugyo outbreak; reported lower CFR than many Zaire ebolavirus outbreaks.
details: Use for clinical presentation, CFR prior and comparison with current outbreak; historical context not real-time data.

title: The basic reproductive number of Ebola and effects of public health measures: Congo and Uganda
key_message: Estimated R0 for Congo 1995 and Uganda 2000 Ebola outbreaks and impact of control timing.
details: Historical modelling baseline for intervention-timing sensitivity; not species-specific for current BVD outbreak.

title: Automated epidemiological research screening: Bundibugyo/Ebola transmission dynamics, forecasting and importation risk
source: Europe PMC / PubMed / medRxiv screening plan
key_message: The dashboard is configured to screen for reproduction number, serial interval, incubation period, CFR, transmission dynamics, forecasting, importation risk and contact-tracing studies relevant to Bundibugyo virus disease and filovirus outbreaks.
details: This row documents the screening scope. GitHub Actions can append candidate records from Europe PMC queries to epidemiological_research_candidates.csv; manual review is recommended before promotion to the curated tracker.
relevance: Ensures epidemiological analyses are separated from product R&D updates.
screening_query: (Bundibugyo OR Ebola OR ebolavirus) AND (transmission dynamics OR model OR forecasting OR importation OR reproduction number OR serial interval OR case fatality)
journal_scope: Epidemiology, Emerging Infectious Diseases, Eurosurveillance, Lancet Infectious Diseases, PLOS NTD, PLOS Pathogens, eLife, Science, Nature Medicine, JID, CID, BMC Infectious Diseases, IJID

title: Prior outbreak epidemiology of Bundibugyo virus disease: clinical severity, CFR and transmission context
key_message: Historical Bundibugyo outbreaks remain essential for priors on incubation, CFR, clinical spectrum and transmission heterogeneity when current outbreak data are sparse.
details: The automated screening emphasizes Uganda 2007 and DRC 2012 Bundibugyo publications, plus comparative ebolavirus studies, to inform current epidemiological models and parameter priors.
relevance: Provides priors for transmission models and severity assessment.
screening_query: Bundibugyo virus disease Uganda 2007 DRC 2012 epidemiology CFR transmission
journal_scope: Clinical Infectious Diseases, Journal of Infectious Diseases, Emerging Infectious Diseases, PLOS ONE, PLOS NTD
